results.title: The microwave auditory phenomenon - IEEE Xplore
excerpts: Abstract: When human subjects are exposed to rectangular pulses of microwave radiation, an audible sound occurs which appears to originate from within or behind the head.

results.title: Microwave-induced hearing: some preliminary theoretical ...
excerpts: Pulsed microwaves impinging on the head of animals and man produce auditory sensations. Theoretical analyses indicate a series of resonant frequencies occurring inside the head.

results.title: Microwave auditory effect- a comparison of some possible ...
excerpts: The results show that while all three (radiation pressure, strictive force and thermal expansion) are capable of producing the phenomenon, the stress resulting from thermal expansion may be so great that it masks the effect of the others completely.

results.title: Health effects: hearing microwaves: the microwave auditory ...
excerpts: The microwave auditory phenomenon, or microwave hearing effect, pertains to the hearing of short pulses of modulated microwave radiation at high peak power by humans and laboratory animals. Research into this effect is examined.

results.title: The Microwave Auditory Effect | IEEE Journals & Magazine ...
excerpts: This paper reviews the research in humans and animals leading to scientific documentations that absorption of a single microwave pulse impinging on the head may be perceived as an acoustic zip, click, or knocking sound. A train of microwave pulses may be sensed as buzz, chirp, or tune by humans.

results.title: Maintenance therapy after first‐line therapy for ovarian cancer: Quantitative effectiveness - PMC
excerpts:
Section Title: Maintenance therapy after first‐line therapy for ovarian cancer: Quantitative effectiveness > REFERENCES
Content:
Niraparib first‐line maintenance therapy in patients with newly diagnosed advanced ovarian cancer: final overall survival results from the PRIMA/ENGOT‐OV26/GOG‐3012 trial. Ann Oncol. 2024;35(11):981‐992. doi: 10.1016/j.annonc.2024.08.2241 [ [DOI](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.annonc.2024.08.2241) ]  [PMC free article ] [ [PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39284381/) ] [ [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar_lookup?journal=Ann%20Oncol&title=Niraparib%20first%E2%80%90line%20maintenance%20therapy%20in%20patients%20with%20newly%20diagnosed%20advanced%20ovarian%20cancer:%20final%20overall%20survival%20results%20from%20the%20PRIMA/ENGOT%E2%80%90OV26/GOG%E2%80%903012%20trial&author=BJ%20Monk&author=MP%20Barretina%E2%80%90Ginesta&author=B%20Pothuri&volume=35&issue=11&publication_year=2024&pages=981-992&pmid=39284381&doi=10.1016/j.annonc.2024.08.2241&) ]
Section Title: Maintenance therapy after first‐line therapy for ovarian cancer: Quantitative effectiveness > REFERENCES
Content:
doi: 10.1016/j.ejca.2021.08.023 [ [DOI](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ejca.2021.08.023) ]  [PMC free article ] [ [PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32749942/) ] [ [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar_lookup?journal=Eur%20J%20Cancer&title=Efficacy%20of%20maintenance%20olaparib%20for%20patients%20with%20newly%20diagnosed%20advanced%20ovarian%20cancer%20with%20a%20BRCA%20mutation:%20subgroup%20analysis%20findings%20from%20the%20SOLO1%20trial&author=P%20DiSilvestro&author=N%20Colombo&author=G%20Scambia&volume=157&publication_year=2021&pages=415-423&pmid=32749942&doi=10.1016/j.ejca.2021.08.023&) ]
Section Title: Maintenance therapy after first‐line therapy for ovarian cancer: Quantitative effectiveness > Susana M Campos , MD, MPH > Stephanie V Blank , MD
Content:
For patients with ovarian, peritoneal, and fallopian tube carcinoma, completing first‐line treatment, maintenance therapy aims to stave off the risk of recurrence with the hope it will improve overall survival. Multiple trials, including the Gynecologic Oncology Group GOG‐0218 trial, <sup>1</sup> , <sup>2</sup> which investigated the vascular endothelial growth factor inhibitor (VEGFi) bevacizumab; SOLO1 <sup>3</sup> , <sup>4</sup> , <sup>5</sup> (olaparib; ClinicalTrials.gov identifier [NCT01844986](https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT01844986) ); PRIMA <sup>6</sup> , <sup>7</sup> (niraparib; (ClinicalTrials.gov identifier [NCT02655016](https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT02655016) ); and PAOLA‐1 <sup>8</sup> (bevacizumab plus olaparib (ClinicalTrials.gov identifier NCT24777644) have uniquely and independently played pivotal roles in shaping the role of maintenance therapy in this patient population.

results.title: Computational modeling investigation of pulsed high peak ...
excerpts: The potential of these stress waves to initiate injury mechanisms may warrant further investigation. The thermoelastic stress wave mechanism is exemplified by the well-known microwave auditory effect (MAE).
